Definition: The adductor sesamoid of the thumb, located at the metacarpophalangeal (MP) joint of the thumb. Its ossification marks SMI stage 2+.
The left (lateral) margin of the epiphysis of the middle phalanx of the third finger.
The right (medial) margin of the epiphysis of the middle phalanx of the third finger.
The left (lateral) margin of the distal end of the diaphysis of the middle phalanx of the third finger.
The right (medial) margin of the distal end of the diaphysis of the middle phalanx of the third finger.
The most distal point on the MP3 diaphysis, closest to the epiphysis — used to measure the fusion gap.
The most proximal point on the MP3 epiphysis, closest to the diaphysis — used to measure the fusion gap.
The most distal point on the PP3 (proximal phalanx 3) diaphysis, defining the fusion gap.
The most proximal point on the PP3 epiphysis, defining the fusion gap.
The most distal point on the radius diaphysis, closest to the epiphysis — for late-stage fusion assessment.
The most proximal point on the radius epiphysis, defining the distal fusion gap for late SMI staging.
